content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

January 29, 1990

David Cameron
2100-A G Street
Sacramento, CA 95816

Re: Your Request for Informal
Assistance
Our File No. I-89-701

Dear Mr. Cameron:

You have requested advice concerning the campaign provisions
of the Political Reform Act.1/ Your letter states only a general
question. Therefore, we consider it to be a request for informal
assistance pursuant to Regulation 18329(c).2/

QUESTION

What is the filing requirement for a committee primarily
formed to support the qualification of a ballot measure when the
committee's qualification effort extends over two years?

CONCLUSION

The period covered in the initial campaign report required of
a committee primarily formed to support the qualification of a
ballot measure begins on January 1 of the year in which the
qualification effort began through seven days prior to the date
when the statement is due. The statement is due not later than 21
days after the date any petitions are filed or 21 days after the
deadline for filing petitions, whichever is earlier.

_________________________

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2/ Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Government Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs. Section
18329(c)(3).)

428 J Street, Suite 800 ● P.O. Box 807 ● Sacramento CA 95804-0807 ● (916)322-5660

ANALYSIS

A committee formed or existing primarily to support the
qualification of a ballot measure must file its first campaign
statement disclosing contributions received and expenditures made
in connection with the qualification effort 21 days after any
petitions are filed, or 21 days after the deadline for filing
petitions, whichever is earlier. The closing date for the period
is seven days prior to the deadline for filing the statement.
(Section 84200.5(f).)

When the qualification effort extends over two calendar
years, the period covered in the 21-day post-qualification
campaign statement begins on January 1 of the year the
qualification effort began. (Section 82018.) Thus, the statement
will disclose activity which occurred in two calendar years.

After filing the 21-day post-qualification statement, the
committee may have additional filing obligations depending on its
activities. Enclosed is the 1989 "Information Manual On Campaign
Disclosure Provisions of the Political Reform Act" which describes
committee disclosure requirements.

Please do not hesitate to contact me at (916) 322-5662 if you
have additional questions.

Sincerely,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

By: Lynda Cassady
Political Reform Consultant

Enclosure

FPPC

Dec 20 8:25 AM '89                David Cameron
                                   2100-A G Street
                                   Sacramento, CA 95816


December 19, 1989


Fair Political Practice Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California  95804

Dear Gentlepersons:

I want to confirm that the first campaign report that needs
to be filed for an initiative is 21 days after any petitions
are filed, or 21 days after the deadline for filing
petitions, whichever is earlier.

My specific question is, does this rule apply even when the
initiative is kicked off in the fall of one year and the
petitions are filed in the spring of the next year?

If so, does the first report cover the whole 12 months of
the first calendar year and from January 1st of the second
calendar year through seven days prior to the date when the
statement is due?

Sincerely,

David Cameron

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

December 22, 1989


David Cameron
2100-A G Street
Sacramento, CA  95816

Re:  Letter No. 89-701

Dear Mr. Cameron:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act
was received on December 20, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices
Commission.  If you have any questions about your advice request,
you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly.  Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21
working days if your request seeks formal written advice.  If more
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response
to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to the
information needed.  If your request is for informal assistance,
we will answer it as quickly as we can.  (See Commission
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).)

You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Very truly yours,


Jeanne Pritchard
Chief Technical Assistance
and Analysis Division
